content.text:
pluginManagement {
    plugins {
        id 'org.springframework.boot' version "${springBootVersion}"
        id 'io.spring.dependency-management' version "${springDependencyManagementVersion}"
        id 'org.asciidoctor.jvm.convert' version "${asciidoctorConvertVersion}"
        id 'io.spring.javaformat' version "${springJavaFormatVersion}"
    }
}

rootProject.name = 'spring-boot-java-template'

include 'core:core-enum'
include 'core:core-api'
include 'storage:db-core'
include 'tests:api-docs'
include 'support:logging'
include 'support:monitoring'
include 'clients:client-example'

# SpringBoot Java Template [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. # **Modules** ## Core > Only describe the core:core-api module and ignore the rest of the module groups because they are implementation noise. Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. ### core:core-api It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. ### core:core-enum This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules.
## Clients Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. ### clients:clients-example This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`.
## Storage Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. ### storage:db-core This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`.
## Support Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. ### support:logging This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. ### support:monitoring This module supports monitoring of services.
## Tests Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. ### tests:api-docs This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently.
# Dependency Management All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`.
# Runtime Profiles ## local This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. ## local-dev This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. ## dev This profile exists for deploying Development environments. ## staging This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. ## live This profile exists for deploying Live environments.
# Test Tasks & Tags ## test This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. ## unitTest This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. ## contextTest This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. ## restDocsTest This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. ## developTest This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests.
# Recommended Preferences ## Git Hook This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. ``` $ git config core.hookspath .githooks ``` ## IntelliJ IDEA This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. ``` // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA ``` If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) --- # Supported By

data.content: Now let me explore each of the module directories to understand their internal structure.
Let me read a few key files to understand the build configurations and the main application class.

content.text:
### Application version ###
applicationVersion=0.0.1-SNAPSHOT

### Project configs ###
projectGroup=io.dodn.springboot
javaVersion=25

### Plugin dependency versions ###
asciidoctorConvertVersion=4.0.5
springJavaFormatVersion=0.0.47

### Spring dependency versions ###
springBootVersion=4.0.0
springDependencyManagementVersion=1.1.7
springCloudDependenciesVersion=2025.1.0

### External dependency versions ###
sentryVersion=8.27.1

bootJar.enabled = true
jar.enabled = false

dependencies {
    implementation project(":core:core-enum")
    implementation project(":support:monitoring")
    implementation project(":support:logging")
    implementation project(":storage:db-core")
    implementation project(":clients:client-example")

    testImplementation project(":tests:api-docs")
    
    implementation 'org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-webmvc'
}

data.content:
## Module Structure Summary

This repository is a **Spring Boot multi-module Gradle project** (`spring-boot-java-template`) designed as a starting template for a Spring Boot service. The project is organized into 5 top-level module groups containing 7 submodules, each with a distinct responsibility.

---

### 1. `core` — Business Logic & API

The heart of the application. Contains two submodules:

#### `core:core-enum`
- **Purpose:** Holds shared enums used by `core-api` and other modules.
- **Content:** A single enum (`ExampleEnum`) in the package `io.dodn.springboot.core.enums`.
- **Build:** An empty `build.gradle` (no dependencies — pure Java code).

#### `core:core-api`
- **Purpose:** The **only executable module** in the project. It is the Spring Boot application entry point.
- **Key traits:**
  - Contains `CoreApiApplication.java` (annotated with `@SpringBootApplication` and `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan`).
  - `bootJar.enabled = true` — produces a runnable JAR.
  - Packages follow a **layered domain structure**:
    - `controller/` — REST endpoints (`HealthController`, `ExampleController` at `/api/v1/...`)
    - `domain/` — Business logic (`ExampleService`, domain models `ExampleData`, `ExampleResult`)
    - `support/error/` — Unified exception handling (`CoreException`, `ErrorCode`, `ErrorMessage`, `ErrorType`)
    - `support/response/` — Standardized API response wrapper (`ApiResponse`, `ResultType`)
    - `config/` — Async configuration and exception handlers
- **Dependencies:** Aggregates all other modules (`core-enum`, `storage:db-core`, `clients:client-example`, `support:logging`, `support:monitoring`, `tests:api-docs` for testing) plus `spring-boot-starter-webmvc`.

---

### 2. `clients` — External System Integration

#### `clients:client-example`
- **Purpose:** Demonstrates HTTP communication with external services using **Spring Cloud OpenFeign**.
- **Key traits:**
  - Spring Cloud OpenFeign + Apache HC5 client + Micrometer integration.
  - Contains `ExampleApi` (Feign client interface), `ExampleClient` (wrapper), `ExampleConfig`, DTOs, and a result model.
  - Has its own YAML configuration (`client-example.yml`).
  - Test application (`ClientExampleTestApplication`) with a context test.

---

### 3. `storage` — Data Persistence Layer

#### `storage:db-core`
- **Purpose:** Persistence layer using **Spring Data JPA** with MySQL (and H2 for testing).
- **Key traits:**
  - Depends on `core:core-enum`.
  - Contains JPA entities (`ExampleEntity`, `BaseEntity` with common fields), a Spring Data `ExampleRepository`, and JPA/DataSource configuration classes (`CoreDataSourceConfig`, `CoreJpaConfig`).
  - Runtime dependencies: `mysql-connector-j` and `h2` (for tests).
  - Has its own database configuration (`db-core.yml`) and integration test (`ExampleRepositoryIT`).

---

### 4. `support` — Cross-Cutting Support

#### `support:logging`
- **Purpose:** Centralized logging configuration with distributed tracing support and Sentry integration.
- **Key traits:**
  - Depends on `spring-boot-starter-opentelemetry` (OpenTelemetry for tracing) and `sentry-logback`.
  - Provides per-profile Logback configurations: `logback-local.xml`, `logback-local-dev.xml`, `logback-dev.xml`, `logback-staging.xml`, `logback-live.xml`.
  - Configuration: `logging.yml`.

#### `support:monitoring`
- **Purpose:** Application monitoring and metrics.
- **Key traits:**
  - Depends on `spring-boot-starter-actuator` and `micrometer-registry-prometheus`.
  - Configuration: `monitoring.yml`.

---

### 5. `tests` — Test Utilities

#### `tests:api-docs`
- **Purpose:** Convenience base class for **Spring REST Docs** (API documentation via AsciiDoc).
- **Key traits:**
  - Exposes `RestDocsTest` base class in package `io.dodn.springboot.test.api`.
  - Depends on `spring-boot-restdocs` and `spring-restdocs-mockmvc`.
  - Used as a test dependency by `core:core-api`.

---

### Gradle Build Configuration

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `settings.gradle` | Declares all 7 submodules and the root project name. |
| `build.gradle` (root) | Applies `java-library`, `spring-boot`, `dependency-management`, `spring-javaformat`, and `asciidoctor`. Configures common dependencies (JUnit 5, AssertJ, Mockito, boot config processor). Defines test task types: `unitTest`, `contextTest`, `restDocsTest`, `developTest`. |
| `lint.gradle` | Applies Spring Java Format plugin to all subprojects. |
| `gradle.properties` | Central version management (Spring Boot 4.0.0, Spring Cloud 2025.1.0, Java 25, Sentry 8.27.1, etc.). |

---

### Dependency Graph (Simplified)

```
core:core-api  (executable)
 ├── core:core-enum
 ├── storage:db-core ──→ core:core-enum
 ├── clients:client-example
 ├── support:logging
 ├── support:monitoring
 └── tests:api-docs (test only)
```

---

### Summary Table

| Module Group | Submodule | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Core** | `core:core-api` | Main Spring Boot application, REST API, domain logic, error handling, response wrapping |
| **Core** | `core:core-enum` | Shared enums consumed by `core-api` and other modules |
| **Clients** | `clients:client-example` | External HTTP API integration via OpenFeign |
| **Storage** | `storage:db-core` | JPA/MySQL persistence, entities, repositories, datasource config |
| **Support** | `support:logging` | Centralized logging, OpenTelemetry distributed tracing, Sentry |
| **Support** | `support:monitoring** | Actuator health endpoints, Prometheus metrics |
| **Tests** | `tests:api-docs` | Spring REST Docs base class for API documentation |

The template is designed for **early-project productivity** — it encourages separation of concerns while keeping everything in a single build. As the project grows, each module group is intended to be split into more fine-grained submodules (e.g., more domain services under `core`, more storage backends under `storage`).
